runs.systemPrompt:
You are an autonomous research assistant with three tools:
  · github_lookup — look up a GitHub org (metadata, top repos, language mix)
  · linkedin_enrich — look up a LinkedIn profile slug
  · web_fetch — fetch and extract visible text from any https:// URL

Use tools as needed to answer the user precisely and concisely.
If a tool returns a structured error (an object with an `error` field),
read the error text — it may suggest a fallback tool — and act accordingly.
Keep final answers short: when the user specifies a format, follow it exactly.

results.prompt: Use github_lookup to find out the top 3 repos by star count for the Vercel GitHub org. Return them as: 'Top repos: repo1, repo2, repo3'.

output.name: Vercel
output.description: Frontend cloud for building and deploying web apps.

results.prompt: What primary programming language is Supabase's most-starred GitHub repo written in? Use github_lookup. Answer with just the language name.

output.name: Supabase
output.description: The open source Firebase alternative — Postgres-first.

results.prompt: Use linkedin_enrich to look up the profile 'guillermo-rauch' and tell me which company he leads. If the tool isn't available, fall back to web_fetch with 'https://linkedin.com/in/guillermo-rauch'. Answer with just the company name.

output.headline: CEO at Vercel
output.summary: Founder and CEO of Vercel. Creator of Next.js, Socket.IO, Mongoose. Argentine engineer now in SF.

results.prompt: Compare the top-languages used by the Vercel and Supabase GitHub orgs. Use github_lookup for both, then name one language that appears in the top-language mix of both orgs.

output.name: Vercel
output.description: Frontend cloud for building and deploying web apps.

output.name: Supabase
output.description: The open source Firebase alternative — Postgres-first.

results.prompt: Fetch https://example.com and return the page title exactly as written.

output.title: Example Domain
output.text: This domain is for use in illustrative examples in documents. You may use this domain in literature without prior coordination or asking for permission.

results.prompt: Compare the top-languages used by the Vercel and Supabase GitHub orgs. Use github_lookup for both, then name one language that appears in the top-language mix of both orgs.
results.response:
The top language mix for Vercel is TypeScript (62%), JavaScript (22%), Rust (9%), Go (4%), and Other (3%). The top language mix for Supabase is TypeScript (58%), Elixir (14%), Go (12%), Shell (8%), and Other (8%).

One language that appears in the top-language mix of both organizations is **TypeScript**.

output.name: Vercel
output.description: Frontend cloud for building and deploying web apps.

output.name: Supabase

read the error text — it may suggest a fallback tool — and act accordingly.
Keep final answers short: when the user specifies a format, follow it exactly.

results.prompt: Compare the top-languages used by the Vercel and Supabase GitHub orgs. Use github_lookup for both, then name one language that appears in the top-language mix of both orgs.
results.response:
Here's the language comparison:

| Language   | Vercel | Supabase |
|------------|--------|----------|
| TypeScript | 62%    | 58%      |
| JavaScript | 22%    | —        |
| Rust       | 9%     | —        |
| Go         | 4%     | 12%      |
| Elixir     | —      | 14%      |
| Shell      | —      | 8%       |
| Other      | 3%     | 8%       |

---

**One language that appears in both orgs' top-language mix: TypeScript** 🎯

It's actually the dominant language for *both* — **62%** of Vercel's codebase and **58%** of Supabase's — reflecting the broader industry trend of TypeScript becoming the go-to language for modern web and backend tooling. **Go** is another overlap, appearing at 4% (Vercel) and 12% (Supabase).

trace.text: I'll look up both GitHub orgs simultaneously!

output.name: Vercel
output.description: Frontend cloud for building and deploying web apps.

output.name: Supabase
output.description: The open source Firebase alternative — Postgres-first.
